# Alex Gerlitz - AI Automation / Backend / Platform Engineer

> I build backend-owned AI workflow and operations systems for real businesses: RAG, transcript and call analysis, approval flows, CRM/ERP/API adapters, FastAPI/PostgreSQL backends, Docker/CI, runbooks, and recovery paths.

This is the AI-readable proof index for my public GitHub/LinkedIn review route. It is intentionally short: start with the strongest proof path, then inspect the repositories and verification routes that show how the work is built, tested, operated, and handed off.

## Primary Proof Route

- [DriveDesk AI Operator proof route](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/drivedesk-proof-route.html): backend-owned AI workflow proof for RAG, transcript analysis, privacy redaction, lead scoring, approvals, CRM handoff, audit, retries, and adapter boundaries.
- [AI Backend Proof Pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/ai-backend-proof-pack.html): compact route through AI workflow, backend/platform, integration, DevOps, live proof, verification, and resume evidence.
- [AI Automation Role Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/ai-automation-role-fit.html): focused role-fit route for backend-owned AI workflows, RAG/context retrieval, structured outputs, approvals, CRM/API handoff, tests, logs, docs, and supportable runbooks.
- [Enterprise Readiness](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/enterprise-readiness.html): international employer-grade review route for architecture, state ownership, integration contracts, English-first docs, async proof paths, specialist review evidence, international hiring readiness, specialist backend-owned AI workflow signal, reliability, privacy, observability, CI, runbooks, and handoff quality.
- [DriveDesk Flagship Platform](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/flagship-platform.html): larger backend/platform direction around roles, records, workflows, adapters, audit/outbox, admin/operator surfaces, DevOps, observability, backups, and recovery paths.
- [Hiring decision route](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/hiring-decision.html): one-minute role fit, why to shortlist me, risk reduction, strongest proof, first-month ownership, and contact route.
- [LinkedIn Recruiter Packet](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/linkedin-recruiter-packet.html): search filters, 30-second fit, employer trigger matrix, shortlist scorecard, ownership signal, featured work samples, proof order, message triggers, decision-ready reply, expected first result, shortlist signal, risk reduction, AI-assisted execution proof, and international remote-team proof routing. It also exposes structured recruiter packet data.
- [AI Ops Employer Trigger Proof](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/ai-ops-workflow-kit/blob/main/docs/EMPLOYER_TRIGGER_PROOF.md): maps employer triggers to concrete repo evidence, verification commands, first-result proof, and backend-owned workflow boundaries.
- [Recruiter Preferences](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/recruiter-preferences.html): Open-to-Work alignment for job titles, remote work mode, employment types, recruiter search keywords, proof stack by search intent, proof order, and first contact fit. It also maps the LinkedIn skills search surface to public proof and exposes structured data for recruiter/search tooling.
- [Decision-Ready Contact](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/decision-ready-contact.html): minimum first context, fit read, risk check, smallest responsible first slice, proof-backed offer route, and next step.
- [Start conversation](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/start-conversation.html): recruiter shortcut, role-target links, PDF resume, proof-backed project offer route, ownership-first context, and the fastest contact path.
- [Contact routes](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/inbound-response.html): search intent first links for AI Automation/RAG, Backend/Python/Platform, CRM/ERP/API Integration, DevOps/Reliability, proof-backed fixed-scope offers, plus decision-ready context and response shape.
- [Selected proof projects](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/projects.html): repo-level evidence across DriveDesk AI Operator, DriveDesk Core, AI Ops Workflow Kit, DeployMate, and MPlusForm.
- [Remote role targets](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/role-targets.html): search-fit roles mapped to proof, first-month ownership, and direct review routes.
- [Work with me](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/work-with-me.html): remote role and fixed-scope project route with best immediate starts, fit filter, proof links, and contact path.
- [Proposal-ready Services request](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/services.html): LinkedIn Services request shape with proof-backed offer router, current workflow, systems involved, one observable success condition, constraints, and what must not break.
- [Skill evidence map](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/skill-evidence.html): skills mapped to public proof for AI automation, backend/platform, CRM/ERP integration, DevOps, and AI-assisted execution discipline.
- [Verification pack](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/verification-pack.html): CI, live smoke, public demo, evidence links, and proof freshness.
- [AI Backend Proof Pack Markdown](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/AI_BACKEND_PROOF_PACK.md): GitHub-readable proof order for recruiters, hiring managers, and technical reviewers.
- [Enterprise Readiness Markdown](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/ENTERPRISE_READINESS.md): GitHub-readable matrix for enterprise/backend/platform review signals.
- [LinkedIn Services Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/linkedin-service-page-fit.html): public HTML fit filter for broad LinkedIn Services labels around backend/platform ownership, AI workflow, integration, data, DevOps, and internal operations.

## Employer Decision Path

- 2-minute fit check: DriveDesk proof route, project role signal map, and PDF resume.
- 10-minute technical review: AI Ops reviewer snapshot, DriveDesk Core, and verification pack.
- 30-minute specialist review: privacy boundary, live owner proof, DeployMate proof, current CI/smoke routes, and handoff evidence.

## Best-Fit Work

- Remote-only backend, platform, DevOps, AI automation, integration, and internal operations roles for international and distributed teams.
- Fixed-scope projects with one messy workflow, one success condition, and a first working slice with tests, logs, docs, and a handoff path.
- Strongest fit: backend-owned AI workflows, CRM/ERP/API adapters, RAG systems, Telegram/operator approvals, internal admin tools, deployment/recovery workflows, and production-style proof.
- LinkedIn Services fit filter: Web Development or Custom Software Development requests are a fit only when they include backend/platform, AI workflow, integration, DevOps, or internal-operations ownership.
- Not my target right now: onsite-only work, pure prompt/content tasks, isolated brochure/static websites, student/course assignments, generic mobile/ecommerce apps without backend or integration ownership, and vague work where success cannot be verified.

## Search-Fit Role Targets

- Headline: AI Automation / Backend / Platform Engineer | RAG, LLM workflows, CRM/ERP integrations, FastAPI, Docker, DevOps | DriveDesk AI Operator.
- Best-fit titles: Back End Developer, Python Developer, AI Automation Engineer, LLM Workflow / RAG Engineer, Platform Engineer, DevOps Engineer, Integration Engineer, Internal Tools Engineer.
- LinkedIn recruiter search alignment: remote-only full-time roles where Python, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Docker, GitHub Actions, RAG, Vector Databases, CRM/ERP/API integration, Systems Integration, DevOps, OpenAPI, n8n, Telegram, or pgvector appear in the role.
- Work mode: remote-only full-time roles or concrete fixed-scope projects with backend/platform/integration ownership.
- First-month ownership: ship one business workflow slice with backend-owned state, tests, logs, docs, deployment path, and handoff.

## Pinned Proof Repositories

- [DriveDesk Core](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/drivedesk-core): FastAPI/PostgreSQL operations platform foundation with tenants, RBAC, audit/outbox, adapters, OpenAPI, Docker, pytest, CI, docs, and public demo.
- [AI Ops Workflow Kit](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/ai-ops-workflow-kit): document intake, privacy redaction before RAG/approval/CRM handoff, deterministic RAG quality eval, RAG retrieval, transcript analysis, approvals, Telegram callback, dry-run Bitrix CRM handoff, outbox, CI, and smoke checks.
- [DeployMate](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/deploymate): self-hosted Docker deployment control panel direction with FastAPI, Next.js, PostgreSQL, SSH runtime tooling, health checks, logs, release gates, and runbooks.
- [MPlusForm](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/MPlusForm): validation-boundary and trust-model automation proof with untrusted local files, optional Python sync, server-approved snapshots, Windows operations docs, Lua addon boundary, and [public verification run](https://github.com/AlexGerlitz/MPlusForm/actions/runs/28296918568).

## Fast Contact

- Message me first when there is one messy workflow, one risky integration boundary, or one backend/platform/DevOps slice that should become testable, logged, documented, and handed off.
- First reply promise: fit read, risky assumptions, smallest responsible first slice, proof path to inspect, and the right next route: remote role, fixed-scope project, technical review, or no-fit.
- Shortest proof path: LinkedIn Recruiter Packet -> Hiring Decision -> DriveDesk Proof Route -> PDF resume.
- First useful result: a verified slice with backend-owned state, tests, logs, docs, and a handoff route.
- Message reason router: use Start conversation to choose remote role hiring, workflow/project, or technical proof review before sending LinkedIn context.
- Recruiter shortcut: for a remote role, start with LinkedIn Recruiter Packet, Recruiter Preferences, Hiring decision route, Remote role targets, Skill evidence, and the PDF resume. Best match: backend/platform, AI automation, CRM/ERP/API integration, internal tools, or DevOps ownership.
- [LinkedIn profile](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alex-gerlitz-a659ab3bb/)
- [LinkedIn Services](https://www.linkedin.com/services/page/3153b734507b8a60ab/)
- [LinkedIn Services Fit](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/linkedin-service-page-fit.html)
- [Remote AI Automation Services](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/services.html): fast first message and proposal-ready route for one remote role or one messy workflow, one success condition, systems involved, constraints, what must not break, and a working-slice outcome.
- [Remote role targets](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/role-targets.html)
- [Work with me](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/work-with-me.html)
- [Start conversation](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/start-conversation.html)
- [Decision-Ready Contact](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/decision-ready-contact.html)
- [Inbound brief](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/intake-brief.html): role, project, proof-backed offer route, success condition, systems involved, constraints, and verification context.
- [Contact routes](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/inbound-response.html)
- [PDF resume](https://alexgerlitz.github.io/AlexGerlitz/output/pdf/alex-gerlitz-remote-ai-automation-resume.pdf)

## Review Notes

- I use AI-assisted execution as an engineering accelerator, but I keep ownership of architecture, state, tests, logs, verification, deployment, docs, and shipped quality.
- The main signal is not no-code node wiring. The signal is backend-owned state, deterministic RAG quality eval, privacy boundaries, audit, retries, idempotency, integration contracts, smoke checks, and runbooks.
